title: Stelis toscanoi
intro: Stelis toscanoi, also known as Stelis, is a type of orchid plant species native to Mexico and Central America, belonging to the family Orchidaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which may aid in wound healing, reducing fever, and relieving respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Stelis toscanoi include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Stelis is often prepared as a decoction or tincture to treat various health conditions, and it is also used in traditional remedies for coughs, sore throats, and skin irritations.
taxonomy: Stelis toscanoi is classified in the family Orchidaceae, which includes the majority of orchid species. Within the Orchidaceae family, it is placed in the subfamily Epidendroideae. The genus Stelis, to which Stelis toscanoi belongs, is a group of epiphytic orchids native to the Americas. The species Stelis toscanoi is further classified within its genus due to shared characteristics such as its small size and delicate floral structure. It is often grouped with other species of Stelis in botanical studies due to similarities in their morphology.
